techs.name: Agriculture
techs.description: Bread first; empires can quarrel about philosophy after supper.

techs.name: Workshop Standards
techs.description: Gauges, braces, and cutter-sets stop frontier building from looking accidental.

techs.name: Merchant Charters
techs.description: Someone must be licensed to overcharge for nails.

techs.name: Cartography
techs.description: The frontier becomes much easier to rule once it has been insultingly well measured.

techs.name: Warbands
techs.description: A disciplined mob is still a mob, but it starts winning more often.

techs.name: Boiler Alchemy
techs.description: The kettle hisses, the glass clouds, and ore starts behaving like a respectable science.

techs.name: Field Rigging
techs.description: Straps, harnesses, and portable frames turn baggage into warfare.

techs.name: Stoneworks
techs.description: Stone is the first polite way of saying no.

techs.name: Deep Prospecting
techs.description: If the ground objects, dig deeper until it apologizes.

techs.name: Irrigation
techs.description: Canals are what happen when paranoia meets agriculture.

techs.name: Assembly Guilds
techs.description: Once every craft has a foreman, even pelts become industry.

techs.name: Signal Fires
techs.description: A beacon is just diplomacy with brighter consequences.

techs.name: Aether Moorings
techs.description: Brass winches and humming pylons make a port look properly expensive.

techs.name: Brass Drillwork
techs.description: Once the brass fittings match, the army starts pretending it was always professional.

techs.name: Ceramic Stores
techs.description: Clay keeps better secrets than wood, especially from rats.

techs.name: Royal Mint
techs.description: Once the crown stamps metal, every market starts behaving like law.

techs.name: Crystal Lattices
techs.description: A good lattice turns raw crystal from a rock into an argument.

techs.name: Mercantile Ledgers
techs.description: Accountants are how empires weaponize boredom.

techs.name: Survey Corps
techs.description: Men with chains and notebooks are often more dangerous than cavalry.

techs.name: Bastion Walls
techs.description: Unlocks Iron Bastion upgrades for existing Forts and keeps +10% settled defense.

techs.name: Siege Towers
techs.description: Unlocks Siege Tower upgrades for existing Siege Outposts.

techs.name: Convoy Logistics
techs.description: Wheels, manifests, and shouting make an empire move faster than dignity should allow.

techs.name: Aether Bridge
techs.description: The sea becomes considerably less final once the bridge crews stop fearing electricity.

techs.name: Census Records
techs.description: Once the state can count everyone, it begins developing opinions.

techs.name: Dockworks
techs.description: Every respectable harbor eventually becomes a machine with tides.

techs.name: Beacon Towers
techs.description: Taller beacons mean fewer surprises and better excuses.

techs.name: Overload Protocols
techs.description: If the gauges turn red, that only means the machine is trying harder.

techs.name: Quartermaster Corps
techs.description: Armies march on paperwork, then complain about the rations.

techs.name: Deep Extraction
techs.description: The earth gives more when leaned on with proper machinery.

techs.name: Cipher Bureaus
techs.description: Spies improve dramatically once given clerks and a budget.

techs.name: Aether Engineering
techs.description: Once enough crystal is involved, even geography becomes negotiable.

techs.name: Banking
techs.description: Gold behaves best when given marble floors and stern supervision.

techs.name: Provincial Ministries
techs.description: Eventually every empire invents offices and regrets it immediately.

techs.name: Rail Networks
techs.description: Once the rails arrive, distance becomes an accounting problem.

techs.name: Grand Synthesis
techs.description: At full pressure the retort stops accepting the world's categories and starts drafting better ones.

techs.name: Starforged Steel
techs.description: Unlocks Thunder Bastion upgrades for existing Iron Bastions.

techs.name: Grand Cartography
techs.description: The map is no longer a record of the world; it is a threat against it.

techs.name: Monument Cities
techs.description: Some cities stop being settlements and start becoming arguments with skylines.

techs.name: Imperial Exchange
techs.description: If every market in the world hates you, the project is functioning correctly.

techs.name: Standing Army
techs.description: Unlocks Dread Tower upgrades for existing Siege Towers.

techs.name: Sky Docks
techs.description: Once the harbor learns to levitate, the neighbors stop sleeping soundly.

techs.name: Resonance Grid
techs.description: The air itself starts reporting suspicious activity.

techs.name: Aether Towers
techs.description: Build enough of them and the empire starts sounding dangerous at night.

techs.name: Aegis Dome
techs.description: Nothing says enlightened government like a city under glass.

techs.name: Worldbreaker Cannon
techs.description: Why capture a district gracefully when you can simply remove it from geology?

techs.name: Astral Dock
techs.description: If the heavens will not visit the empire, the empire will launch a drydock at them.

techs.name: Seedline Granaries
techs.description: Steam-pressed silos hum at night and the towns around them grow visibly before breakfast.

techs.name: Exchange Levy Writs
techs.description: Once the Imperial Exchange learns to issue writs, the rest of the world becomes a tributary by accident.

techs.name: Worldbreaker Ignition
techs.description: Calibrating the cannon for a single shot took longer than building it.
